strapi/strapi#10160.body:
## Bug report

### Describe the bug

**strapi-provider-email-nodemailer** package is ignoring nodemailer mail/config attributes like headers.
Therefore it is not possible to send custom SMTP headers as documented here.
https://github.com/nodemailer/nodemailer/blob/master/examples/custom-headers.js#L19

This happens because the email attributes are whitelisted but the 'headers' attribute is missing in that whitelist there https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/master/packages/strapi-provider-email-nodemailer/lib/index.js#L19

Fixed whitelist 
```javascript
  const emailFields = [
  'from',
  'replyTo',
  'to',
  'cc',
  'bcc',
  'subject',
  'text',
  'html',
  'attachments',
  'headers'         // <-- missing attribute name
];
```

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Configure strapi to use strapi-provider-email-nodemailer
2. Send an email with custom headers like 'X-SOME-CUSTOM-HEADER': 'SOME VALUE'
3. Check the received email source does't have the header above

### Expected behavior

Received email source should have custom headers in message

### Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

### Code snippets

If applicable, add code samples to help explain your problem.

### System

- Strapi version: 3.5.4

### Additional context

Add any other context about the problem here.
strapi/strapi#10160.title: strapi-provider-email-nodemailer SMTP custom headers not being sent

strapi/strapi#17731.body:
## Bug report

### Required System information

<!-- Please ensure you are using the Node LTS version (v16 or v18 or v20) -->
<!-- Strapi v3 is not supported unless it is a critical/high security issue -->

- Node.js version: 18.17.1
- NPM version: 9.8.1
- Strapi version: 4.12.4
- Database: sqlite
- Operating system: Ubuntu Linux 22.04
- Is your project Javascript or Typescript: N/A

### Describe the bug

I accidentally closed my Strapi browser window and lost an hour's work in progress. I was on my way to press Save but that's by the by.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

- In Content Manager, work on a long edit to content in a Rich Text field.
- Close tab (Ctrl-V and Ctrl-W are so near each other in Dvorak :man_shrugging: ).
- Cry.
- Hit Ctrl-Shift-T, hoping that the form would contain the previous text.
- Cry again.
- Open issue here because surely this is a bug.

### Expected behavior

I expected a dialog or something similar, perhaps trapping `beforeunload` to stop me shooting myself in the foot.

Edit: I just did a test; I note that clicking the back button with unsaved data does bring up a dialog like the following. However, this does not pop up when closing a window.

![image](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/assets/295571/4f58ffdb-b5ef-4a0e-bd76-625cb5e10215)

### Additional context

Funnily enough I created https://feedback.strapi.io/feature-requests/p/autosave-when-editing-fields today because I could feel that something like this might happen.

Based on this, I can't use Strapi for editing content now; I'll have to edit elsewhere then paste in, which somewhat defeats the purpose of the Rich Text Field.
strapi/strapi#17731.title: Accidentally closing Strapi window => losing unsaved work

## Bug report

### Required System information

- Node.js version:  v18.17.0 win32 x64
- NPM version: npm v10.0.0 / yarn v1.22.19 <-- I use yarn
- Strapi version: 4.15.5-alpha.4
- Database: postgresql
- Operating system: Windows x64
- Is your project Javascript or Typescript: Typescript

### Describe the bug

When trying to bulk publish a content entry i receive an error.
It works completely fine just pressing Publish directly on the entry, but bulk publish does not work.

It only happens when a media property is set to required and nested within a component.


### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to "Content Type Builder"
2. Create new component "Thumbnail" add property "image" as single media `required`
3. Create new collection type "Test" add property "component" and choose "Thumbnail" and set `required`
4. Go to "Content Manager" select "Test" and create new entry.
5. Set image in thumbnail component and save
6. Click on "Select All" and click on "Publish"
7. See error

### Expected behavior

Since the property Thumbnail.image is set, the publish function should not report an error.
This only happens when the media property is required and nested within a component.

### Screenshots

Component: 
![](https://i.imgur.com/YfYoU1G.png)

Collection Type:
![](https://i.imgur.com/WYZtlxI.png)

Content Manager:
![](https://i.imgur.com/zH0ulo0.png)

*Error:*
![](https://i.imgur.com/Q74xaoc.png)

strapi/strapi#20895.body: ## Bug report ### Required System information - **Node.js version**: 18.17.0 - **NPM version**: yarn/1.22.19 - **Strapi version**: 5.0.0-rc.7 - **Database**: sqlite - **Operating system**: darwin-arm64 - **Project type**: Typescript ### Describe the bug The locales in the LocalePicker component are not ordered alphabetically. **Further improvement suggestion:** Can we consider providing users with the option to make `LocalePicker` a design system [`Combobox`](https://design-system.strapi.io/?path=/docs/inputs-combobox--docs)? This enhancement would significantly improve the user experience (UX) for projects with a large number of locales. Currently, it is possible to achieve this with injection, but it requires copying helpers like [`usei18n`](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/v5/main/packages/plugins/i18n/admin/src/components/LocalePicker.tsx#L7) and [`useGetLocalesQuery`](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/v5/main/packages/plugins/i18n/admin/src/components/LocalePicker.tsx#L8). This approach may complicate keeping the local Strapi instance up to date with the i18n plugin. ### Steps to reproduce the behavior 1. Navigate to any content type having localisation enabled. 2. Observe the order of the locales in the LocalePicker dropdown. ### Expected behavior The locales should be ordered alphabetically in the LocalePicker component to facilitate easier navigation, especially for projects with a large number of locales. ### Screenshots ### Code snippets N/A ### Additional context N/A

### Describe the bug

In the blocks editor, lists can be indented but not unindented. Some of the time <kbd>shift</kbd> + <kbd>tab</kbd> actually indents instead of un-indenting!

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Add a rich test (blocks) field

Tab indents a list item to the right on an RTL system. <kbd>shift</kbd> + <kbd>tab</kbd> should indent to left.

strapi/strapi#22076.body:
### Why should we do it?
Strapi currently supports [multiple file extensions](https://docs.strapi.io/user-docs/media-library/adding-assets) and displays a `File` icon to illustrate their assets cards. There's an exception for PDFs, for which we show a specific icon named `FilePdf`.

![CleanShot 2024-11-05 at 17 05 19@2x](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5cbaa002-ff48-4c20-9959-7f9843739093)

Our new icon library (Phosphor) also has icons for XLS, ZIP and CSV files. In those cases, it would be great to display these icons instead of the default `File` icon, as we do for PDFs.

### Warning
We'll first need to add the icons to our [design system](https://github.com/strapi/design-system), as explained in [this issue](https://github.com/strapi/design-system/issues/1813).
strapi/strapi#22076.title: Specific icons for XLS, ZIP and CSV files

### Describe the bug

May be fixed in more recent version of v5, but the `alternativeText` and `caption` field for files are set to a 255 character limit on postgres (and possibly other datastores).  

Based on some quick research I did, it would appear that there's actually no browser/spec/screen reader limit for `alt` text for image, and that seems like the most-likely place for this value to be used.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the media library or upload a file directly into a content-type
2. Edit the alternative text or caption to be longer than 255 characters
3. Submit the changes
4. See error

### Expected behavior

That both the alternative text and caption fields support text longer than 255 characters

### Bug Description

I have an internationalized content-type called blogpost in my strapi instance. There are 2 available locales, `sv` and `en`.
It has fields `title`, `text` and `images`, and I'm using a short text field for `slug` (I want localized entries to share slug so I couldn't use UID).

When querying the rest api for entries of both locales, using the parameter `locale="*"`, the pagination data is returned with correct counts for `page` and `pageSize`, but `pageCount` and `total` are reported as 0, even though data was returned by the query.

Example of broken query, this runs in my sveltekit project:
```ts
let query = qs.stringify(
  {
    locale: "*",
    fields: [
      'slug',
      'title',
      'documentId',
      'createdAt',
      'updatedAt',
      'text',
      'locale'
    ],
    sort: 'createdAt:desc',
    pagination: {
      page: 1,
      pageSize: 2
    }
  },
  {
    encodeValuesOnly: true,
  })
let url = PUBLIC_API_URL + `/api/blogposts?${query}`;
let posts = await (await fetch(url)).json()

console.log(posts);
```

The output of the `console.log` call is:
```ts
{
  data: [
    {
      id: 53,
      documentId: 'rzhm8dgw9yhyt78r48vrvyii',
      slug: 'whats-up-1',
      title: 'whats up',
      createdAt: '2025-01-18T23:16:28.827Z', 
      updatedAt: '2025-01-18T23:16:46.480Z', 
      text: [Array],
      locale: 'sv'
    },
    {
      id: 50,
      documentId: 'sfrh1kpwtlkfh6k8fuy6s9gx',
      slug: 'whats-up',
      title: "what's up",
      createdAt: '2025-01-18T23:15:41.866Z',
      updatedAt: '2025-01-18T23:15:41.866Z',
      text: [Array],
      locale: 'sv'
    }
  ],
  meta: { pagination: { page: 1, pageSize: 2, pageCount: 0, total: 0 } }
}
```

If I remove the `locale="*"` parameter, or set it to either "en" or "sv", the pageCount and total are reported correctly.

Working query:

```ts
let query = qs.stringify(
  {
    //locale: "*",
    fields: [
      'slug',
      'title',
      'documentId',
      'createdAt',
      'updatedAt',
      'text',
      'locale'
    ],
    sort: 'createdAt:desc',
    pagination: {
      page: 1,
      pageSize: 2
    }
  },
  {
    encodeValuesOnly: true,
  })
let url = PUBLIC_API_URL + `/api/blogposts?${query}`;
let posts = await (await fetch(url)).json()

console.log(posts);
```

output of `console.log`:

```ts
{
  data: [
    {
      id: 53,
      documentId: 'rzhm8dgw9yhyt78r48vrvyii',
      slug: 'whats-up-1',
      title: 'whats up',
      createdAt: '2025-01-18T23:16:28.827Z', 
      updatedAt: '2025-01-18T23:16:46.480Z', 
      text: [Array],
      locale: 'sv'
    },
    {
      id: 50,
      documentId: 'sfrh1kpwtlkfh6k8fuy6s9gx',
      slug: 'whats-up',
      title: "what's up",
      createdAt: '2025-01-18T23:15:41.866Z',
      updatedAt: '2025-01-18T23:15:41.866Z',
      text: [Array],
      locale: 'sv'
    }
  ],
  meta: { pagination: { page: 1, pageSize: 2, pageCount: 3, total: 6 } }
}
```

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable internationalization with 2 locales (I use `en` and `sv`)
1. Create a content-type with internationalization enabled on it
1. Enable the public user to `find` and `findOne` on this content-type
1. Create an entry of this content-type, and create and publish both locales for that entry
1. Query the REST API for entries of **all** locales for the content-type. This is done by including the `locale=*` parameter in your query. 

Observed behaviour: The query correctly returns the entries (in `data`), but the accompanying pagination data (in `meta`) is wrong.
If you created 1 entry and published 2 locales, the returned pagination data of this query is:
`{ page: 1, pageSize: 25, pageCount: 0, total: 0 }` 

### Expected Behavior

Expected behaviour: If you created 1 entry and published 2 locales, the returned pagination data of this query should be:
`{ page: 1, pageSize: 25, pageCount: 1, total: 2 }`

### Bug Description

The documentation plugin seems to be reading the OpenAPI specification JSON files from the `src/` directory, rather than from the `dist/src/` directory. In standard production deployments, the `src/` directory is not included on the deployment servers, making it unavailable. As a result, when users access the documentation page, the documentation settings page, or the OpenAPI endpoint, they encounter an error.

This issue appears to have been introduced by [this pull request](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/21950). Previously, the documentation would be generated into the `src/` directory upon startup, regardless of the environment. However, this behavior no longer occurs in production environments. Despite that, the plugin continues to attempt to read from `src/` instead of from the correct `dist/src/` directory.


### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a Strapi project.
2. Add the documentation plugin.
4. Build and run the server in production mode.
3. Remove the `src` directory.
5. Access the documentation page.

### Expected Behavior

The server should read the OpenAPI JSON files from `dist/src/` instead of `src/`.

Keep the source as modified, when I publish a translation I don't publish the modified source. We can ex: hotfix the translated page, yet the changes inside the source don't need to be published.

_No response_

### Bug Description

When an S3-compatible provider does not return the protocol (`https`) as part of the `Upload.Location` property, Strapi is not adding `baseUrl` to the final URL. This is a problem when using a CDN url that is different from that of the bucket.

Strapi already contains code that handles S3 providers that don't include the protocol as part of the Location prop, as introduced by https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/14654:

https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/7c7ba817d00aeac2e9f750e6693dc8e51a8a2659/packages/providers/upload-aws-s3/src/index.ts#L120-L124

When the `baseUrl` property was introduced, this else block was not updated to handle it properly.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Add the `@strapi/provider-upload-aws-s3` plugin to a Strapi instance
2. Set up a provider that does not return the protocol in the `Location` prop (I have been using Hetzner Object Storage, but DigitalOcean Spaces is reported to also have an issue according to https://github.com/strapi/strapi/pull/14654)
3. Upload a file
4. Resulting URL of the file does not consider `baseUrl`.

### Expected Behavior

If a `baseUrl` is provided, even if the S3 provider does not return the protocol/https as part of the `Location` prop, it should use it correctly in combination with the `fileKey` to create a valid accessible URL. If a `baseUrl` is not provided, then it should fall back to the current behavior.

### Logs
This is a log of the UploadCommandOutput. Notice the Location value without a protocol.

```shell
{
  '$metadata': {
    //
  },
  Bucket: 'bucketName',
  ETag: 'x',
  Key: 'file.png',
  Location: 'region.your-objectstorage.com/bucketName/file.png'
}
```

When seeing records in default language (English) in a multilingual setup with one-to-many relations, child record dropdowns still show values in the secondary language. ### Node Version 20.18.1 ### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version 10.5.2 ### Strapi Version 5.5.0 ### Operating System Windows 11 ### Database MySQL ### Javascript or Typescript Typescript ### Bug Description Hello,
I’m facing an issue related to role-based permissions in Strapi. A client needs a user role (e.g. "Publisher") that can read and publish content, but not update it. This setup works correctly for collection types, where the user can publish without needing update permissions. However, for single types, when the "Update" permission is disabled, attempting to publish results in a 403 Forbidden error. As soon as the "Update" permission is enabled, publishing works as expected. Publishing single types is currently tied to update permissions, which isn't consistent with how it works for collection types. Thanks! ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a new role (e.g. "Publisher") with the following permissions for a single type: * Read * Publish * (Do not enable Update) 2. As a user with this role, go to the single type entry. 3. Click the "Publish" button. 4. Observe that a "Forbidden" error appears. 5. Log in as a Super Admin and update the role to also allow the Update permission. 6. Log back in as the same user. 7. Open the same single type entry and click "Publish" again. 8. Notice that publishing now works as expected. ### Expected Behavior The publisher role should be able to publish single types without needing to have the update permission ### Media [https://streamable.com/wjwdwe]() ### Confirmation Checklist - [X] I have checked the existing [issues]() for duplicates. - [X] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct]().

### Bug Description

The globe icon intended to indicate whether a field is localizable appears to be malfunctioning. Regardless of a field’s actual localization setting, the **icon always displays as if the field is localizable**. Based on our previous experience using Strapi v4, non-localizable fields were visually distinguished with a globe icon crossed by a red line. This distinction now appears to be missing.

**Impact:**
This issue can cause confusion for editors managing multilingual content, as they cannot easily distinguish which fields support localization.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a component in the Content-Type Builder.
2. Set one field within the component to be localizable (note this field).
3. Set another field in the same component to be non-localizable (note this field as well).
4. Save the model and restart/rebuild Strapi.
5. Open the Content Manager.
6. Add the affected component to an entry (e.g., a page).
7. Observe the globe icons next to both fields.

**Observed Behavior:**
Both fields display the same globe icon, indicating they are localizable—even though one of them is explicitly set as non-localizable.

### Expected Behavior

The UI should clearly differentiate between localizable and non-localizable fields. For example, non-localizable fields should show a globe icon with a red strikethrough or another clear visual indicator, as seen in earlier versions of Strapi.

### Logs

```shell

```

### Node Version

22.16.0

### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version

10.9.2

### Strapi Version

5.16.0

MacOS

### Database

SQLite

### Javascript or Typescript

Typescript

### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Bug Description

There's currently an issue in the edit view of the content manager when localization is enabled making the statuses of other versions of a collection type not available in the localization filter:

From an existing locale (all statuses good):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69c9d454-e041-471b-b822-c1acb473f9b4)

From a new locale (we loose all statuses of other locales):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85170641-0695-4397-bff1-a16047370355)

It's a backend issue and after investigating a bit it's mainly because when we create a new locale for an existing object of the content manager, the object document (from useDocument) in LocalePickerAction (packages/plugins/i18n/admin/src/components/CMHeaderActions.tsx from line 110) is empty (because not created already for the locale we want to provide). Because of that, we don't have the list of existing localizations defined for the document.

Console.log of documentId + document:
- When we are on an existing locale:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4137e249-b82c-4d31-bebe-5fcc663abd40)

_Thanks to the property localizations in document, we can map the locales and them to display their status in the list._

- When we are on a locale we just created (that doesn't exist yet):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b63f6d46-8a6a-4118-8415-1c2e488212ce)

_Since the document is an empty object, we don't have the localizations attribute and so we can't map them in the list of locales._

ℹ️ For the filter, we're loading the list of locales documents defined in the Settings of the project and we map with the existing locales defined for the current document to see if a locale already exist and also to display its status (Draft / Published / Modified).

💡 Instead of loading the current document only, we could add and use a method to retrieve all localizations of a document (without any filter, just get the list and that's it) to use it here for the mapping.

### Steps to Reproduce

Video reproducing the bug:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b310fa73-a246-4cb5-b90f-1ea055e01926

Steps to reproduce:

- Go to the content manager
- Choose any collection type that has a localized field
- Create a new entry
- Fill a few fields and Save / Publish
- Click on the localization list (right top, next to the little globe icon on the left side)
- Create a new locale
- Try to open again this filter of locales
- You will see that the base entry doesn't have the Published status anymore and the option is back to + Create {LANG} locale

### Expected Behavior

No matter if we create a new locale of are on an existing one, when we open the list of locales, we should be able to see the existing versions / locales of the current document.

### Confirmation Checklist

- [x] I have checked the existing [issues](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues) for duplicates.
- [x] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/develop/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

strapi/strapi#23835.body:
### Node Version

20.14.0

### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version

10.9.1

### Strapi Version

5.16.1

### Operating System

MacOS

### Database

MariaDB

### Javascript or Typescript

Javascript

### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Bug Description

When I publish an item, but a required field is not filled in, I get the following error message:

> Your current permissions prevent access to certain required fields. Please request access from an administrator to proceed.

However, I am a super admin, so this statement cannot be correct. When searching through the code, I was puzzled by the relevant part of the code:

https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/44d360536462e83bfc95c01fd21384d2a42d321f/packages/core/content-manager/admin/src/pages/EditView/components/DocumentActions.tsx#L704

The problem for me seems to be that when determining `hasUnreadableRequiredField` there are incorrect values in `canReadFields` when fields come from components.

In my example, the field "metadata" is defined on this item:
```
    "metadata": {
      "type": "component",
      "repeatable": false,
      "pluginOptions": {
        "i18n": {
          "localized": true
        }
      },
      "component": "landingpage.meta",
      "required": true
    },
```

canReadFiles contains in my case:
```
[
    "translate",
    "title",
    "navigationTitle",
    "path",
    "foregroundColor",
    "backgroundColor",
    "metadata.description",
    "metadata.opengraphTitle",
    "metadata.opengraphDescription",
    "blocks",
    "registry.sitemapShow",
    "registry.navigationShow",
    "registry.navigationRoot",
    "registry.navigationImportant",
    "registry.navigationWeight",
    "overrides.landingpage",
    "overrides.landingpageStart",
    "overrides.landingpageEnd"
]
```

When checking the fields, the `metadata` field is checked, but this is not contained in `canReadFiles`. Hence the wrong error.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an item with a component in its definition
2. Publish an item while some required fields are not filled  

### Expected Behavior

On error the page should scroll to the problematic field and not display a misleading error dialog.

### Logs

```shell

```

### Node Version 22.15.0 ### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version 10.9.2 ### Strapi Version 5.15.0/5.23.0 ### Operating System Windows 11 ### Database MySQL ### Javascript or Typescript Typescript ### Reproduction URL *No response* ### Bug Description Hello,
I’ve come across another issue related to the Releases functionality in Strapi. Here’s the scenario: I have two collection types: Page and Section.
A Page has a relation field to multiple Sections, and a Section has a relation field back to a Page.
I create a completely new Page and a new Section, both as drafts.
I add both entries to a release and then deploy the release.
At this point, Strapi shows both the Page and the Section as published (the admin UI marks them as published, and in the DB I can see two entries for each – one without published_at and one with it).
However, when I query the new Page (via client or Postman), the Section field is empty.
The only way I’ve found to resolve this is to manually open the Section in the Strapi admin, make a small change (e.g. add a space), and re-publish it. After that, the Section correctly appears in the client/Postman response. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a **collection type** `Page` with: * `title` (string) * `sections` (relation → many Sections to one Page) 2. Create a **collection type** `Section` with: * `title` (string) * `page` (relation → one Page to many Sections) 3. In the admin, create a **new Section** as **Draft** (do not publish). 4. Create a **new Page** as **Draft** and link the Section in the `sections` relation (do not publish). 5. Create a **Release**, add both the Draft **Page** and Draft **Section** to the Release. 6. **Deploy** the Release. 7. Confirm in the admin UI that both entries now show as **Published**. 8. From a client (or Postman), fetch the published Page via **GraphQL** with relations populated ### Expected Behavior The sections field should **include** the related Section. ### Logs ```shell ``` ### Code Snippets *No response* ### Media *No response* ### Additional information *No response* ### Confirmation Checklist - [X] I have checked the existing [issues]() for duplicates. - [X] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct]().

### Node Version

20.18.0

### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version

Yarn 4.9.2

### Strapi Version

5.12.4

### Operating System

MacOS

### Database

PostgreSQL

### Javascript or Typescript

Typescript

### Bug Description

When a user has multiple roles with overlapping permissions but on different locales, the `useI18n` hook does not merge the locales correctly. As a result, the Locale Picker doesn't show authorized locales from all roles.

So in the code below from `LocalPicker` component line 21, the hook will return locales related to create action from one role and locales related to read action from one role:
`const { hasI18n, canRead, canCreate } = useI18n();`

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create two roles with the same permission on the same collection type, but each restricted to different locales::

- Role A → `canRead` on Articles for locale `en`
- Role B → `canRead` on Articles for locale `fr`

2. Assign both roles to the same user.
3. Login with that user and go to the Content Manager → Articles.
4. The Locale Picker only shows en (or fr), but not both.

### Expected Behavior

The Locale Picker should display both locales (`en`, `fr`).

### Code Snippets

In this example of permissions array, the user has two roles, each granting the same read permission on the Articles collection type, but for different locales:
```
[
  {
    "subject": "api::article.article",
    "action": "plugin::content-manager.explorer.read",
    "properties": { "locales": ["en"] }
  },
  {
    "subject": "api::article.article",
    "action": "plugin::content-manager.explorer.read",
    "properties": { "locales": ["fr"] }
  }
]
```
As a result, the Locale Picker displays only one locale (either `en` or `fr`), because the `useI18n` hook does not merge locales from multiple roles with the same action. It assumes that there will always be one entry for each action.

### Node Version 20.19.4 ### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version npm 10.8.2 ### Strapi Version 4.25.23 ### Operating System MacOS ### Database PostgreSQL ### Javascript or Typescript Javascript ### Reproduction URL [https://github.com/AlbertoCalvoRubio/my-strapi-project]() ### Bug Description When a simple migration is done (change one attribute type, e.g title from string to text), Strapi detects that some unique indexes have changed when they have not.
This is happening when comparing the old and new schemas. For example, for a table `banners_components`, when the indexes are compared, in the index `banners_unique`, the columns of the previous index are `[ "entity_id", "component_id", "component_type", "field"]` and for the new index the columns are `[ "entity_id", "component_id", "field", "component_type"]`. The order is changing and the comparison is done by this line of code `!_.isEqual(oldIndex.columns, index.columns)` where order is important. This results in dropping and recreating the unique indexes, which impacts performance at startup for large tables. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a Strapi project with some content types and add components to them. 2. Run Strapi so that the schema is created. 3. Edit the schema.json of one of the content types, changing a `string` attribute to be of type `text`. 4. Run `npm run start` with database config `debug` enabled and see the drop + create indexes (it can also be seen with the debugger, e.g vscode, adding a breakpoint in `node_modules/@strapi/database/dist/index.js` line 1310). ### Expected Behavior Indexes are not recreated when there are no changes related to them. ### Logs ```shell [ { sql: 'alter table "public"."banners_components" drop constraint "banners_unique"', bindings: [] }, { sql: 'alter table "public"."banners_components" add constraint "banners_unique" unique ("entity_id", "component_id", "field", "component_type")', bindings: [] } ] [ { sql: 'alter table "public"."ctas_components" drop constraint "ctas_unique"', bindings: [] }, { sql: 'alter table "public"."ctas_components" add constraint "ctas_unique" unique ("entity_id", "component_id", "field", "component_type")', bindings: [] } ] ``` ### Code Snippets *No response* ### Media *No response* ### Additional information After investigating the @strapi/database package, I believe that the bug might also come from comparing the indexes from the previous schema, read from `db.dialect.schemaInspector.getSchema()`, which reads the schema from `information_schema` and other pg tables instead of the one already stored in the table `strapi_database_schema`.
This could be fixed by either one of the following: * By using the old schema already stored in `strapi_database_schema`, if there is no specific reason to read the schema from PostgreSQL internal tables. * Compare the index columns without comparing the position of the elements in the arrays. ### Confirmation Checklist - [X] I have checked the existing [issues]() for duplicates. - [X] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct]().

### Node Version

v22.12.0

### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version

NPM 10.9.0

### Strapi Version

5.24.1

### Operating System

Strapi Cloud

### Database

PostgreSQL

### Javascript or Typescript

Typescript

### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Bug Description

### Summary
The Content Manager endpoints over-populate the `localizations` relation and include heavy `meta.availableLocales` by default. This produces large payloads and slow admin UI, especially with many locales and deep relations. Limiting the populated localization fields and disabling `availableLocales` where not needed yields substantial improvements.



### Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a localized collection type with dynamic zones/components and many entries. Ensure `i18n` and Draft & Publish are enabled. Create ~20 different documents with lots of content and translations
2. In the CM:
   - List view: Loads very slowly and the payload of the API response can be multiple MB's. For each document it contains all the translations with all the content in them which leads to the large payload.
   - Single entry view: Documents contain deeply nested data for translated versions of the document and `meta.availableLocales` object again with deeply nested data. This is most likely not needed

### Actual Behavior (example from our production data)
- List view payloads: ~3 MB
- Single item payloads: ~700 KB
- Admin UI loads slowly.
- With DB debug logging enabled, we see about 700 db queries to load the List View with 10 documents

### Expected Behavior

- Admin responses should not deep-populate `localizations` by default.
- `availableLocales` should be opt-in or minimal, and only included when explicitly requested by the client (e.g., via a query parameter), not in every entry fetch.
- probably other localization specific improvements which don't fetch translations unless needed



### Logs

```shell

```

### Code Snippets

_No response_

### Media

_No response_

### Additional information

### Proposed Improvements
1. Default populate rules for `localizations` in CM endpoints should return minimal identifiers (`id`, `locale`, `documentId`) unless deeper data is explicitly needed/requested.
2. Make `meta.availableLocales` opt-in (e.g., controlled by a query parameter) and avoid computing/sending it by default.
3. Consider a configuration option in CM to control localization population depth and metadata inclusion if there's a use case for it.

### Results (in case the proposal is implemented):
- List view: 3 MB → ~100 KB
- Single item: 700 KB → ~100 KB
- Noticeably faster CM load and potentially persist times. -> a lot happier customers since they don't need to wait

### Notes
- We can share a minimal reproduction or diffs illustrating the controller-level changes that produced these improvements.
- A simple way to reproduce this is to use one of the example projects, enable ~10-20 locales, create the documents, manually fill the translations and inspect the response body of the api calls that these views execute. You can see the data returned contains all the translated content which is overkill and has massive impact in case of large documents with many translations
- As far as I understand strapi and how it persists on save, all the submitted data is sent and persisted again. Therefore it consumes a lot of ressources on the database although it's not needed since there will never be a change in the localized content like this.

strapi/strapi#24565.body:
### Node Version

22.17.0

### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version

PNPM 10.18.2

### Strapi Version

5.27.0

### Operating System

Windows 11

### Database

SQLite

### Javascript or Typescript

Typescript

### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Bug Description

In Strapi admin's Content-Type builder, it is nearly impossible for a keyboard / screen reader user to manipulate / remove a field after it has been added to a content-type.

- Each item in the list of fields reports as "Sortable" when navigated using the arrow keys, but there is no indication of buttons to perform any action on the field.
- When exploring with the TAB key, extra buttons suddenly exist for "Drag", "Edit", and "Delete" (I assume these appear on hover otherwise, though I can't see to confirm this). However, there is no way to activate these buttons with the keyboard, i.e. pressing the space or enter key does nothing.

The NVDA screen reader has some controls to move the mouse cursor to the current position and perform a left-click, and I got this to work once to open the edit modal for a field, but it is VERY imprecise. Every other attempt either did nothing or opened the modal for the wrong field.


### Steps to Reproduce

1. In a freshly-installed or existing Strapi project, navigate to the Content-Type Builder.
2. Create a new Content-Type or Component or open a previously-created one.
3. If the type is new, create at least one field.
4. Using only the keyboard, navigate to the list of fields with the TAB key until your focus is on one of the three buttons: "Drag", "Edit", or "Delete" (This all might be easier if testing with a screen reader, but I understand some might not want to because of the learning curve).
5. While focused on - for example - the edit button, press the Enter/Return key and observe how the edit field modal does not open. Try the space key as well to the same result.


### Expected Behavior

When focus is on any interactive element on a web page, pressing the Enter or Space key should perform the "click" action. Therefore, the "Edit" modal should open when pressing Enter with focus on the "Edit" button, and the "Drag" and "Delete" buttons should likewise activate. These action buttons should also not be hidden from screen readers at any point, as there isn't really the concept of "hovering" when navigating via the keyboard. Having important content in popovers can create significant accessibility gaps in a UI, so there should probably be an alternative, keyboard-accessible version of the content.


### Logs

```shell

```

### Code Snippets

_No response_

### Media

_No response_

### Additional information

As I know screen reader accessibility bugs are never at the top of any developer's priority list (I have many years of experience to back that bitter, jaded statement), I have considered researching and contributing a fix to this. I am a professional web developer and a subject-matter expert on UI accessibility. However, I thought it best to at least raise the issue and give the teams a chance to respond before diving into a brand new codebase and potentially spending time making a fix that might not even be accepted for one reason or another.

All this to say I am willing to be a resource and even a contributor to get this issue fixed. Strapi has otherwise been astonishingly accessible and is the solution I've been tearing my hair out to find or build for quite some time, but being able to edit the fields I create is vital.

strapi/strapi#24595.body: ### Node Version 20.14.0 ### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version Yarn 4.5.0 ### Strapi Version 5.25.0 ### Operating System Linux (Debian/Ubuntu) ### Database PostgreSQL ### JavaScript or TypeScript TypeScript ### Reproduction URL _No response_ ### Bug Description When saving changes in the Upload Settings page (Settings > Global Settings > Media Library), if the save operation fails due to a network error or server issue, users don't see any error message. The error is only logged to the browser console using console.error(), leaving users unaware that their changes weren't saved. This is inconsistent with how other parts of the upload plugin handle errors - for example, when deleting an asset fails, users get a clear error notification. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Log in to the Strapi admin panel 2. Navigate to Settings > Global Settings > Media Library 3. Toggle one of the settings (e.g., "Responsive friendly upload") 4. Click the "Save" button 5. To simulate a failure, you can: - Open browser DevTools > Network tab - Set throttling to "Offline" before clicking Save OR - Modify the PUT request to an invalid endpoint in the Network tab 6. Click "Save" 7. Notice: No error notification appears in the UI 8. Check the browser console - you'll see the error logged there The issue is in `packages/core/upload/admin/src/pages/SettingsPage/SettingsPage.tsx` at lines 68-70. ### Expected Behavior When the save operation fails, a red error notification should appear at the top of the page with the error message, similar to how other mutations work throughout the admin panel. The notification should use the toggleNotification hook that's already imported in the file (line 22), like this: toggleNotification({ type: 'danger', message: error.message }); This is the pattern used in other error handlers in the same plugin, such as in hooks/useRemoveAsset.ts line 45. ### Logs ```shell Error appears in console only: TypeError: Failed to fetch at SettingsPage.tsx:55 OR Error: Request failed with status code 500 at createError (createError.js:16) at settle (settle.js:17) No user-facing notification is displayed. ``` ### Code Snippets Current buggy code in `packages/core/upload/admin/src/pages/SettingsPage/SettingsPage.tsx` (lines 68-70): ```typescript onError(err) { console.error(err); // Only logs to console } ``` Expected fix: ```typescript onError(err) { toggleNotification({ type: 'danger', message: err.message || formatMessage({ id: 'notification.error' }) }); } ``` For comparison, here's how it's done correctly in the same plugin at `hooks/useRemoveAsset.ts` (line 45): ```typescript onError(error: Error) { toggleNotification({ type: 'danger', message: error.message }); } ``` ### Media This screenshot demonstrates the bug: Left side: Media Library settings page with the Save button visible Right side: Browser console showing multiple "TypeError: Failed to fetch" errors Notice that despite the save operation failing (visible in the console with ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED), there is NO error notification displayed to the user in the UI. The page appears normal with no visual indication that the save failed. The errors shown in the console confirm the mutation's onError callback is being triggered at SettingsPage.tsx:81, but it only calls console.error() instead of showing a user-facing notification. Expected behavior: A red error notification banner should appear at the top of the page, similar to how other mutations in the admin panel handle errors. ### Additional information _No response_ ### Confirmation Checklist - [x] I have checked the existing [issues](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues) for duplicates. - [x] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/develop/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

_No response_

### Bug Description

When saving AI-generated metadata (captions/alt text) in the AI Upload Modal (Media Library), if the save operation fails due to a network error or server issue, users don't see any error message. The error is only logged to the browser console using console.error(), leaving users unaware that their changes weren't saved.

This is inconsistent with how other parts of the upload plugin handle errors - for example, when saving settings fails (fixed in #24595 ), users get a clear error notification.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Log in to the Strapi admin panel
2. Navigate to Media Library
3. Upload an image
4. Click "Generate with AI" to generate metadata
5. Edit the AI-generated caption or alternative text
6. Click "Save" to save the changes
7. To simulate a failure, you can:
   - Open browser DevTools > Network tab
   - Set throttling to "Offline" before clicking Save
      OR
   - Modify the API endpoint to return an error
8. Click "Save"
9. Notice: No error notification appears in the UI
10. Check the browser console - you'll see the error logged there

### Expected Behavior

When the save operation fails, a red error notification should appear at the top of the page with the error message, similar to how other mutations work throughout the admin panel.

The notification should use the toggleNotification hook that's already imported in the file, like this:

toggleNotification({
  type: 'danger',
  message: error.message
});

This is the pattern used in other error handlers in the same plugin, such as in the Settings page (fixed in #24596).

### Logs

```shell
Error appears in console only:

Failed to save asset changes: TypeError: Failed to fetch
    at AIUploadModal.tsx:109

OR

Failed to save asset changes: Error: Request failed with status code 500
    at createError (createError.js:16)
    at settle (settle.js:17)

No user-facing notification is displayed.
```

### Code Snippets

**Current buggy code in** `packages/core/upload/admin/src/ai/components/AIUploadModal.tsx` (line 109):

```typescript
catch (err) {
  console.error('Failed to save asset changes:', err);  // Only logs to console
  return; // Don't close modal on error
}
```

### Media

_No response_

### Additional information

- Affects user experience: users may lose their AI-generated edits without realizing it.
- Inconsistent with Strapi's error handling patterns used elsewhere in the upload plugin.
- Only developers with the console open would notice the failure.
- The modal stays open (good), but users have no idea why the save didn't work.
- Fix: import and use the `useNotification` hook (same approach as in #24595).

- [x] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/develop/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

### Bug Description

The `/auth/refresh` endpoint returns HTTP 400 and "Missing refresh token" if it doesn't see a refresh token in the request body, despite being configured to set refresh token as a httpOnly cookie.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure users-permissions plugin to use the 'refresh' management type:
```
export default () => ({
  'users-permissions': {
    config: {
      jwtManagement: 'refresh',
      sessions: {
        accessTokenLifespan: 120,
        maxRefreshTokenLifespan: 86400,
        idleRefreshTokenLifespan: 604800,
        httpOnly: true,
        cookie: {
          name: 'strapi_up_refresh',
          sameSite: 'lax',
          path: '/',
          secure: false, // true in production
        },
      },
    },
  },
})
```
2. Send a POST request with credentials to `/api/auth/local`, verify that the cookie `strapi_up_refresh` is being set with the httpOnly attribute

3. Send a POST request to `/api/auth/refresh` including the `strapi_up_refresh` cookie.

### Expected Behavior

A 200 response with a JWT in the response body and a fresh refreshToken cookie being set

### Logs

```shell

```

### Code Snippets

The handler for /api/auth/refresh seems to return early if there's no refreshToken in the request body, despite being configured to use httpOnly cookies.

```
    const { refreshToken } = ctx.request.body || {};
    if (!refreshToken || typeof refreshToken !== 'string') {
      return ctx.badRequest('Missing refresh token');
    }
```

strapi/strapi#24931.body:
### What does it do?

Changes several of the text based fields in the file content-type from the upload package from string to text

### Why is it needed?

string based fields in most databases limit the total characters of the value to 255, text (which converts to the `longtext` in some databases) have practically no limit. This is useful especially for URLs where they can easily exceed this limit.

### How to test it?

Double check the database to ensure column type changes without data loss and that you can have file type URLs, file names, ect that exceed 255 characters

### Related issue(s)/PR(s)

fixes: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues/22385
Also fixes another issue but I couldn't find it, cc @christophecvb
strapi/strapi#24931.title: fix: change several file field types from string to text

strapi/strapi#24953.body:
<!--
Hello 👋 Thank you for submitting a pull request.

To help us merge your PR, make sure to follow the instructions below:

- Create or update the tests
- Create or update the documentation at https://github.com/strapi/documentation
- Refer to the issue you are closing in the PR description: Fix #issue
- Specify if the PR is ready to be merged or work in progress (by opening a draft PR)

Please ensure you read the Contributing Guide: https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

## What does it do?

This PR migrates all ID columns (primary keys and foreign keys) from `INTEGER` to `BIGINT` across the database layer. This change ensures better support for large-scale applications and prevents integer overflow issues.
To account for backward compatibility, the `bigint` values are casted in Number up to 2^53, throw an exception if overflowing [MAX_SAFE_INTEGER](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number/MAX_SAFE_INTEGER).

> [!NOTE]
> This PR is currently meant only to trigger CI checks in order to help identify regressions.

## Changes


### Solution approach

#### Database migration

- **Automatic bigint migration**:
  - New internal migration (`5.0.0-06-migrate-ids-from-int-to-bigint`) upgrades:
    - **Primary keys** (`id` columns) in all content-type and internal tables.
    - **Foreign keys** in join/morph tables and relation tables.
  - Handles PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite with dialect-specific logic:
    - Safely drops foreign key constraints before conversion, recreates them afterwards.
    - Uses table recreation where needed (e.g. SQLite’s limited `ALTER TABLE` support).
  - Skips columns that are already bigint-compatible, so it can safely run on existing projects.

#### Transparent bigint handling

- **Internal vs user-defined fields**:
  - Introduces an `internalIntegerId` flag on attributes to mark Strapi-created bigint FKs in join tables.
  - Distinguishes three cases:
    - **ID columns** (`type: 'increments'` or `type: 'bigincrements'`) → exposed as **numbers**.
    - **Internal FK columns** (`type: 'biginteger'` + `internalIntegerId: true`) → exposed as **numbers**.
    - **User-defined `biginteger` attributes** (no flag) → exposed as **strings**.
- **Safety and precision**:
  - Internal IDs/FKs are converted from DB strings to JS numbers and validated against `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`.
  - If an internal ID overflows the safe range or cannot be parsed, Strapi throws a clear error instead of silently corrupting data.
  - User-defined bigintegers are never clamped or validated against `MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` and keep their full string precision.

#### Schema & tooling updates

- **Schema builder & inspector**:
  - New content types and internal tables use `bigincrements` for IDs by default; join-table FKs use `biginteger`.
  - Dialects (MySQL, Postgres, SQLite) recognize bigint PK/FK columns and map them to the correct Strapi types.
- **Diffing & migrations**:
  - Schema diff treats `increments` and `bigincrements` as equivalent to avoid noisy, unnecessary migrations.
  - Internal migration and schema tools are aligned so that once IDs are bigint, subsequent syncs do not try to downgrade/flip types.

### What this changes

- **ID storage in the database**
  - All Strapi‑managed ID columns (PKs and FKs, including join/morph tables and internal tables) are now stored as `BIGINT` in supported databases.
  - A new internal migration walks all tables and:
    - Upgrades integer `id` PKs to bigint.
    - Upgrades integer FK columns that point to those IDs to bigint.
    - Handles FK constraints per dialect (Postgres/MySQL/SQLite) so the migration is consistent and safe.

- **How IDs are exposed in JavaScript**
  - **Internal IDs and FKs (Strapi‑created)**:
    - Still exposed as **numbers** in Node for typical ID ranges.
    - On read, values are validated to be within `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`; anything larger throws a clear error instead of silently losing precision.
  - **User‑defined `biginteger` attributes**:
    - Still exposed as **strings** (unchanged behavior), including very large values beyond JS safe integer range.
    - This preserves existing app logic that treats those values as strings.

- **Schema & metadata model**
  - Internal models (content types, join tables, history/core tables, migrations/schema tables) are updated to:
    - Use `bigincrements` for their `id` fields.
    - Use `biginteger` for internal FK columns.
  - The schema inspector and diffing logic are updated so:
    - PKs using `increments` vs `bigincrements` are treated as equivalent from a migration‑noise point of view.
    - Dialect‑specific quirks (e.g. SQLite’s lack of a real bigint type) are abstracted behind the dialect API.

### Scope in the codebase

- **Database layer**
  - Dialects (MySQL, Postgres, SQLite) schema inspectors and type mapping.
  - Schema builder, diffing, and internal migration storage/schema tables.
  - Field system, especially the bigint field and how it converts DB values to JS values.

- **Metadata and relation generation**
  - Metadata fixtures/expected outputs.
  - Relation/join table builders (including morph and many‑to‑many join tables).

- **Core / CM models and services**
  - Internal models that use Strapi‑managed IDs (history versions, core store, webhooks, etc.).
  - Utilities that transform content types into database models.

- **Tests**
  - New tests around bigint behavior and integration with the transform pipeline.
  - Existing schema/metadata tests updated to expect bigint IDs and FK columns.

### Backwards compatibility

- **Stable for existing apps**:
  - For “normal” ID ranges (i.e. well below \(2^{53}\)), behavior is unchanged: IDs are still numbers in JavaScript.
  - User‑defined `biginteger` fields remain strings and retain full precision; no breaking change there.
- **Stronger guarantees**:
  - If the underlying DB ever stores an internal ID beyond `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`, Strapi will now fail fast with an explicit error, instead of returning a silently corrupted number.

## How to test it?

- Start a Strapi instance on any prior version
- Create content types and content
- Stop the Strapi instance
- Checkout to this version (including the bigint migration)
- Start the Strapi instance
- All content should be migrated to the new bigint ids but the API still delivers number IDs

## Related issue(s)/PR(s)

resolves #24491
strapi/strapi#24953.title: fix(*): migrate IDs from int to bigint

### Bug Description

When attempting to delete an image from the Media Library, clicking Cancel on the confirmation modal still results in the image being deleted.

This behavior causes unintended data loss and indicates that the cancel action does not properly prevent the delete operation from being executed.

I was able to reproduce this consistently on the latest Strapi version (5.33.3) using a fresh local setup, without any custom code or plugins.

### Steps to Reproduce

Start a fresh Strapi project using version 5.33.3

Log in to the Admin Panel

Navigate to Media Library

Select any uploaded image

Click Delete

When the confirmation modal appears, click Cancel

Refresh the Media Library or re-open the folder

### Expected Behavior

The image should remain intact when clicking Cancel on the delete confirmation modal, and no delete request should be executed.

Actual Behavior

The image is deleted, even though the user explicitly clicks Cancel in the confirmation modal.

### Logs

```shell
No errors are logged during this operation.
The deletion occurs silently, without any warnings or failed requests.
```

### Bug Description

When cloning a document with relations to other collections, the relations are not properly transferred when the cloning process requires manual intervention.
If nothing prevents automatic cloning, relations are copied correctly. However, when a field constraint (such as a unique field) prevents automatic cloning, the admin UI redirects to a form page to manually create the document with pre-filled fields. In this scenario, the relations are not set in the form, even though those relations are not the fields causing the initial problem.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a collection type with relations to other collections
2. Add a unique field constraint to one of the fields (e.g., slug, identifier)
3. Create an entry with populated relations
4. Attempt to clone/duplicate this entry
5. Observe that the unique field constraint prevents automatic cloning
6. Check the manual creation form that appears
7. Notice that relations are empty/not pre-filled, while other fields are

### Expected Behavior

When redirected to the manual creation form after a failed automatic clone, all non-conflicting fields (including relations) should be pre-filled with the original document's values. Only the fields causing the conflict (e.g., unique fields) should require manual input.

- tar: ≥ 7.5.8

strapi/strapi#25509.body: ### Node Version 22 ### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version Yarn 4.5.0 ### Strapi Version 5.36.1 ### Operating System MacOS ### Database SQLite ### Javascript or Typescript Typescript ### Reproduction URL https://github.com/strapi/LaunchPad ### Bug Description Renaming a component field label in Content Manager → Configure the view causes the Content Manager list view to crash if the component already contains existing content. After renaming the component label (e.g. from user to User) and saving, navigating back to the Content Manager list view for that content type throws the following error: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'attributes')` The error originates from getMainField inside the admin panel bundle and appears to be related to resolving component attributes when computing the main field for the list view. This only occurs when: - The component already has existing content. - The rename is performed via Configure the view (label change only, not schema change). The Content Manager becomes unusable for that content type until reverted. The issue can be reversed by: 1. Renaming the component label back to its original value. 2. Restarting Strapi. Without restarting Strapi, reverting the label does not immediately fix the issue. ### Steps to Reproduce I could replicate this issue consistently in our LaunchPad demo, but in a blank strapi you would: 1. Create a content type (e.g. Testimonials). 2. Add a component field (e.g. user) with fields such as: - firstname - lastname - job - image 3. Create at least one entry so that content exists in the component. 4. Go to Content Manager → Testimonials → Configure the view. 5. Edit the component field label (e.g. change user to User). 6. Click Finish, then Save. 7. Navigate back to the Content Manager list view for Testimonials. ### Expected Behavior Renaming a component label in Configure the view should only update the UI label and should not: - Break list view rendering - Affect schema resolution - Require a server restart - Cause attribute resolution to fail The Content Manager list view should continue to work normally after saving the updated layout. ### Logs ```shell TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'attributes') at getMainField at convertListLayoutToFieldLayouts at ListViewPage ``` ### Code Snippets _No response_ ### Media ### Additional information This appears to be related to how the admin panel resolves component schemas when computing the mainField for list view rendering. It seems that renaming the label causes a mismatch between stored layout configuration and component schema resolution, resulting in attribute being undefined inside getMainField. The issue only manifests after content exists in the component and requires a Strapi restart to fully recover even after reverting the label. ### Confirmation Checklist - [x] I have checked the existing [issues](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/issues) for duplicates. - [x] I agree to follow this project's [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/strapi/strapi/blob/develop/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

strapi/strapi#25542.body:
### Node Version

24.13.1

### NPM/Yarn/PNPM Version

NPM 11.8.0

### Strapi Version

5.36.1

### Operating System

MacOS

### Database

MySQL

### Javascript or Typescript

Javascript

### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Bug Description

After migrating from Strapi v4 to v5 (5.36.1), the migration produces two categories of broken data. Both are still present after migrating to the latest version.

---

## Bug 1: Draft entries are missing media/file relations

After migration, media fields on **draft** entries are empty in the admin panel. The PUBLISHED tab shows images correctly, but switching to DRAFT shows empty upload fields. This occurs even though draft and published should have identical content immediately after migration.

This affects:
- Direct media fields on content types (e.g. a `cardImage` or `listImage` field)
- Media fields inside components (e.g. an `image` field on a component used in a dynamic zone)

Looking at the database, the migration correctly creates draft content type rows and duplicates first-level component rows with new IDs. However, the `files_related_morphs` table only has entries for the published side — no morph rows are created for the new draft IDs.

**Content type level — direct media fields:**

```
-- Entry is correctly split into published + draft
content_type table:
  id=100  document_id=abc  published_at=2026-01-01  (published)
  id=500  document_id=abc  published_at=NULL         (draft)

-- Morph entry only exists for the published ID
files_related_morphs:
  file_id=42  related_id=100  related_type=api::example.example  field=cardImage  ✓
  (nothing for related_id=500)                                                     ✗
```

**Component level — media inside components:**

```
-- Components are correctly duplicated with different cmp_ids
content_type_cmps:
  entity_id=100 → cmp_id=10  (shared.image, field=topImage)   ← published
  entity_id=500 → cmp_id=20  (shared.image, field=topImage)   ← draft ✓

-- But morph entry only exists for the published component
files_related_morphs:
  file_id=42  related_id=10  related_type=shared.image  field=image  ✓
  (nothing for related_id=20)                                         ✗
```

The component data itself (caption, alt text etc.) is correctly duplicated — only the polymorphic file relation is missing.

In our production database this affected over **40,000 missing morph rows** across ~15,000 content type entries and ~24,000 components.

---

## Bug 2: Published entries are missing relations on nested components

For components nested inside other components, relation fields display correctly on the **DRAFT** tab but are **completely empty on the PUBLISHED** tab. Publishing/republishing does not fix it.

This occurs with structures like:

```
Content type → dynamic zone → Component A → repeatable Component B → relation field
```

Looking at the database, two things are wrong:

**1. Nested components are shared, not duplicated.**

The migration creates separate `cmp_id` values for the first-level components (correct), but the second-level components are never duplicated. Both published and draft parents point to the exact same nested component rows:

```
-- First level: correctly duplicated ✓
pages_cmps:
  entity_id=1   → cmp_id=100  (blocks.list)  ← published
  entity_id=299 → cmp_id=200  (blocks.list)  ← draft

-- Second level: shared, NOT duplicated ✗
components_blocks_lists_cmps:
  entity_id=100 → cmp_id=50  (shared.reference, order=1)  ← published parent
  entity_id=200 → cmp_id=50  (shared.reference, order=1)  ← draft parent, SAME cmp_id
```

This means editing the draft's nested component silently modifies the published version as well. They are not independent.

**2. Relation links point to draft targets only.**

The `_lnk` entries on the shared nested component reference draft target IDs:

```
articles:
  id=100  document_id=def  published_at=2026-01-01  (published)
  id=500  document_id=def  published_at=NULL         (draft)

components_shared_references_article_lnk:
  shared_reference_id=50  article_id=500  ← points to draft article
```

Since Strapi v5 filters relations by publish status, the published view cannot resolve a relation pointing to a draft target. The draft view can, which is why the relation appears on DRAFT but not PUBLISHED.

This affects any content structure where a dynamic zone or component field contains components with nested sub-components that have relation fields.

---

## Summary

| Bug | What's wrong | Visible as |
|-----|-------------|------------|
| 1 | `files_related_morphs` entries not created for draft IDs | Media shows on Published, empty on Draft |
| 2 | Nested components not duplicated, `_lnk` entries point to draft targets | Relations show on Draft, empty on Published |

Both issues stem from the migration not fully traversing the component tree when creating the published/draft split. The first level of data is handled, but polymorphic file relations and deeper component hierarchies are not.

Note: This issue was created with the help of Claude Code for analysing.

### Steps to Reproduce

## Bug 1: Missing media on drafts

1. In Strapi v4, create a content type with a media field (e.g. `cardImage`) and a component with a media field (e.g. a `shared.image` component with an `image` field)
2. Create and publish several entries with images uploaded to both the direct media field and the component media field
3. Migrate to Strapi v5 (the `5.0.0-discard-drafts` migration runs automatically)
4. Open any entry in the admin panel
5. On the PUBLISHED tab, media fields display correctly
6. Switch to the DRAFT tab — media fields are empty

## Bug 2: Missing relations on published nested components

1. In Strapi v4, create a content type with a dynamic zone
2. Add a component to the dynamic zone that contains a repeatable sub-component with a relation field (e.g. `Page → dynamic zone "blocks" → ArticleList component → references (repeatable) → article relation`)
3. Create and publish a page with the dynamic zone populated, including nested components with relations set
4. Migrate to Strapi v5
5. Open the entry in the admin panel
6. On the DRAFT tab, the nested relation fields display correctly
7. Switch to the PUBLISHED tab — the nested relation fields are empty

### Expected Behavior

After the v4→v5 migration, both the DRAFT and PUBLISHED tabs should display identical content — the same media files, the same component data, and the same relations. Specifically:

1. All media/file relations should be duplicated for both the published and draft entry IDs in `files_related_morphs`, including media on components.

2. Nested components (components inside components) should be fully duplicated so that published and draft parents each have their own independent copies. Relation links (`_lnk` entries) on published components should point to published target IDs, and draft components should point to draft target IDs.

```

## Bug Description
After upgrading to Strapi `5.37.0`, `strapi build` fails while building the admin panel.

Vite/Rollup fails to resolve `@radix-ui/react-tooltip` imported by `@strapi/design-system/dist/index.mjs`.

This happens in a Dockerized environment using pnpm (`--frozen-lockfile`).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start from an existing Strapi v5 project using pnpm and Docker.
2. Upgrade Strapi packages to `5.37.0`.
3. Run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
4. Run `pnpm run build` (or Docker build stage running `strapi build`).
5. Observe admin build failure with unresolved import from `@strapi/design-system`.

## Expected Behavior
`strapi build` should complete successfully without requiring app-level direct installation of `@radix-ui/react-tooltip`.

## Logs
```shell
[vite]: Rollup failed to resolve import "@radix-ui/react-tooltip" from "/opt/node_modules/.pnpm/@strapi+design-system@2.1.2.../node_modules/@strapi/design-system/dist/index.mjs".
This is most likely unintended because it can break your application at runtime.
```

## Code Snippets
Project dependencies include:

```json
"@strapi/design-system": "^2.1.2",
"@strapi/icons": "^2.1.2",
"@strapi/strapi": "^5.37.0"
```

Workaround that makes build pass:

```json
"@radix-ui/react-tooltip": "^1.0.7"
```

## Media
N/A

## Additional information
- Build command used in Docker stage:
  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  - `pnpm run build`
- The issue appears to be a dependency resolution/packaging mismatch surfacing under pnpm + Vite strict resolution.

### Bug Description

                                                                                                                                                                      
  The edit view heading for single types renders `schema.info.displayName` as a raw string without passing it through `formatMessage`. This means custom admin        
  translations registered via `config.translations` in `app.js` are ignored for single type page headings, while they work correctly for:
                                                                                                                                                                      
  - The sidebar/left menu (uses `formatMessage` in `LeftMenu.js`)                                                                                                     
  - The collection type list view heading (uses `formatMessage` in `ListViewPage.mjs`)                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                             


### Steps to Reproduce

                                                                                                                                                                      
  1. Create a single type with `displayName: "About"`                                                                                                                 
  2. Add a translation override in `src/admin/app.js`:
     ```js                                                                                                                                                            
     const config = {                                                                                                                                        
       locales: ['de'],                                                                                                                                               
       translations: {                                                                                                                                       
         de: { 'About': 'Über uns' },    
       },                                                                                                                                                             
     }
  3. Switch admin interface to German                                                                                                                                 
  4. Observe: sidebar correctly shows "Über uns", but the edit view page heading still shows "About"                                                         


### Expected Behavior

  The edit view heading should show "Über uns" when the admin locale is German, matching the sidebar behavior.                                                        

### Logs

```shell

```

### Code Snippets

_No response_

### Media

_No response_

### Additional information
                                                                                                                                                             
  **Root cause**
  In packages/core/content-manager/admin/src/hooks/useDocument.ts, the getTitle function returns schema.info.displayName as a plain string for single types:          

```ts
  if (isSingleType && schema?.info.displayName) {                                                                                                                     
    return schema.info.displayName  // raw string, no formatMessage                                                                                          
  }
```
                                                                                                                                                    
The fix should match the pattern used in ListViewPage which correctly wraps it:                                                                                     

```ts
  if (isSingleType && schema?.info.displayName) {                                                                                                                     
    return formatMessage({                                                                                                                                            
      id: schema.info.displayName,       
      defaultMessage: schema.info.displayName,                                                                                                                        
    })                                                                                                                                                       
  }                                      
```

formatMessage (from useIntl) is already imported and available in the useDocument hook.

### Node Version

24

### Package Manager

yarn

### Package Manager Version

11.9.0

### Strapi Version

5.40.0

Other

MySQL

### Javascript or Typescript

### Bug Description

When a content type has a media field that accepts all asset types (images, files, videos, audios), uploading a file with an extension the **browser** doesn't recognize (e.g., `.lottie`) via the content type field's media picker fails with:

> "You can't upload this type of file, only the following types are accepted – images,files,videos,audios"

The same file uploads successfully from the **Media Library page** directly.

In `packages/upload/admin/src/utils/getAllowedFiles.ts`, the filter function rejects any file where `file.mime` produces a falsy first segment:

```js
const getAllowedFiles = (pluralTypes, files) => {
  // ...
  const allowedFiles = files.filter((file) => {
    const fileType = file?.mime?.split('/')[0];
    if (!fileType) {
      return false;  // ← BUG: rejects files with empty MIME
    }
    if (singularTypes.includes('file') && !['video','image','audio'].includes(fileType)) {
      return true;
    }
    return singularTypes.includes(fileType);
  });
  return allowedFiles;
};
```

This creates confusing behavior where the same user can upload the same file from the Media Library page but not from a content type field.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a content type with a media field (single or multiple) that accepts all types (images, files, videos, audios).
2. In the admin panel, open a content entry and click the media field to add an asset.
3. Try to upload a `.lottie` file (or any file with an extension the browser doesn't map to a MIME type).
4. Observe the error: *"You can't upload this type of file, only the following types are accepted – images,files,videos,audios"*
5. Navigate to the Media Library page directly and upload the same file — it succeeds.

### Expected Behavior

Files with an empty/unknown browser MIME type should be allowed through when `"files"` is in the allowed types list, since they are generic files. The server-side MIME validation (`mime-validation.mjs`) already handles content detection properly via `file-type` and extension lookup — the client-side filter should not be more restrictive.

```

### Bug Description

In `packages/core/database/src/entity-manager/index.ts`, the `deleteRelations` method exits early when the database dialect uses foreign keys.

Inside the loop over attributes, the following code is used:

```ts
if (db.dialect.usesForeignKeys()) {
  return;
}
```

This causes the function to stop execution entirely after the first matching attribute instead of continuing to process the remaining attributes.

As a result, some relations may not be properly cleaned up depending on attribute order.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a database that supports foreign keys (e.g. PostgreSQL or MySQL)
2. Create a content type with multiple relational fields
3. Create entries with multiple relations populated
4. Delete an entry
5. Observe that not all relations are properly cleaned up

### Expected Behavior

The function should skip only the current attribute when foreign keys are used, and continue processing the remaining attributes.

Replacing `return` with `continue` would ensure all attributes are handled correctly.

### Logs
